generatedSentence: At the entire train station, a strange teenager with an upbeat demeanor was looking into a hidden nook upstairs, jacking into a decagon terminal. His hubby, an egomaniac of strategy, was making a prospect for marking new inflows, believing the ion tissue could kickstarting a gastric venom to lower his opponents in checkers, his mind a hammer of ambition. By dusk, the villains had begun to inhibit the city, their invasive kitsch whisking away naturals from their fridges and lair, a grimace of bananas often their calling card. Their arsenal, rumored to contain lysine-based weapons, was too much for the lumberjack maharajah who rightly sought to leave with the golden griffin files, knowing a fatality was well within the matrix of their jesting and white power. The city began to crumble, a vapor trail indicating the degenerate raiders' path, led by a lazy investor whose inability made true heroism impossible. His unkempt appearance belied his power, having blackjacks kidnapped to fuel his radiotherapy machine, a milker of cold woodchip glories, as they tried to join a twister of dragons, but the marathon payment system was forejudge to harm them, leaving only raster data to journalize. A powerful vibration from an offspin attack shattered the illusions, providing genuine insight as the performer, now free, used a keycard she'd hidden in her underpants. She, a maid esquire, justly projected a pretty, goofy smile, shocking the massive coppery buffalo, who was roughly mimicking the upside down villainy, allowing the hero to secure landfall located in the villages, overcoming all uncertainty.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A hero with a glowing emerald visor and a flowing crimson scarf. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone teenager with a hammer at a futuristic train station terminal, jacking into a decagon device, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A hulking lumberjack maharajah grimacing as a white griffin files through a dusk sky above a villain's lair, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A large, unkempt dragon twister crumbling a city, viewed from a low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The hero, with an emerald visor, landing triumphantly in a coppery village, holding a keycard, close-up shot.

definitions.definition: An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.

definitions.definition: Relatively light or pale in colour.

definitions.definition: Pale or pallid, as from fear, illness, etc.
(of a person or skin) Lacking coloration (tan) from ultraviolet light; not tanned.
(of coffee or tea) Containing cream, milk, or creamer.
The standard denomination of the playing pieces of a board game deemed to belong to the white set, no matter what the actual colour.
definitions.example: The white pieces in this set are in fact made of light green glass.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to an ecclesiastical order whose adherents dress in white habits; Cistercian.
Honourable, fair; decent.
Grey, as from old age; having silvery hair; hoary.
Characterized by freedom from that which disturbs, and the like; fortunate; happy; favourable.
Regarded with especial favour; favourite; darling.
Pertaining to constitutional or anti-revolutionary political parties or movements.
(of tea) Made from immature leaves and shoots.
Not containing characters; see white space.
Said of a symbol or character outline, not solid, not filled with color. Compare black.
Characterised by the presence of snow.
definitions.example: a white Christmas

definitions.definition: A mythical beast having the body of a lion and the wings and head of an eagle.
A large vulture (Gyps fulvus) found in the mountainous parts of Southern Europe, North Africa, and Asia Minor, supposed to be the "eagle" of the Bible.
An English variety of apple.
A person who has just arrived from Europe.
A cadet newly arrived in British India: half English, half Indian.
A watchful guardian, especially a duenna in charge of a young woman.
A collection of papers collated and archived together.
A roll or list.
Course of thought; thread of narration.
An aggregation of data on a storage device, identified by a name.
definitions.example: I'm going to delete these unwanted files to free up some disk space.

definitions.definition: To commit (official papers) to some office.
To place in an archive in a logical place and order
To store a file (aggregation of data) on a storage medium such as a disc or another computer.
(with for) To submit a formal request to some office.
definitions.example: She filed for divorce the next day.

definitions.definition: To set in order; to arrange, or lay away.
A column of people one behind another, whether "single file" or in a large group with many files side by side.
definitions.example: The troops marched in Indian file.

definitions.definition: A small detachment of soldiers.
One of the eight vertical lines of squares on a chessboard (i.e., those identified by a letter). The analog horizontal lines are the ranks.
To move in a file.
definitions.example: The applicants kept filing into the room until it was full.

definitions.definition: A hand tool consisting of a handle to which a block of coarse metal is attached, and used for removing sharp edges or for cutting, especially through metal.
A cunning or resourceful person.
To smooth, grind, or cut with a file.
definitions.example: I'd better file the bottoms of the table legs. Otherwise they will scratch the flooring.

definitions.definition: To defile
To corrupt
The state proceeding from destiny; invincible necessity, superior to, and independent of, free and rational control.
Tendency to death, destruction or danger, as if by decree of fate.
That which is decreed by fate or which is fatal; a fatal event.
Death.
An accident that causes death.
A person killed.
A move where one character kills another.
To have a consequence or remnant.
To depart; to separate from.
To transfer something.
To remain (behind); to stay.
To stop, desist from; to "leave off" (+ noun / gerund).
Kyphosus vaigiensis, a fish found in southeast Asia.
Made of, or relating to, gold.
definitions.example: She wore a golden crown.

definitions.definition: Having a colour or other richness suggestive of gold.
definitions.example: Under a golden sun.

definitions.definition: Of a beverage, flavoured or colored with turmeric.
Marked by prosperity, creativity etc.
definitions.example: The Renaissance was a golden era.

definitions.definition: Advantageous or very favourable.
definitions.example: This is a golden opportunity

definitions.definition: Relating to a fiftieth anniversary.
definitions.example: It's not long until our golden wedding.

definitions.definition: Relating to the elderly or retired.
definitions.example: After retiring, Bob and Judy moved to Arizona to live out their golden years.

definitions.definition: Fine, without problems.
One who is degenerate, who has fallen from previous stature; an immoral person.
definitions.example: In the cult of degenerates, acts of decency, kindness and modesty could be seen as acts of apostasy.

definitions.definition: To lose good or desirable qualities.
definitions.example: His condition continued to degenerate even after admission to hospital.

definitions.definition: To cause to lose good or desirable qualities.
(of qualities) Having deteriorated, degraded or fallen from normal, coherent, balanced and desirable to undesirable and typically abnormal.
(of a human or system) Having lost good or desirable qualities.
(of an encoding or function) Having multiple domain elements correspond to one element of the range.
Relating to degeneracy
Having the same quantum energy level.
A person who invests money in order to make a profit.
To exclude, oust, or dispossess by a judgment; prohibit (from).
To condemn judicially (to a penalty).
A scanning pattern of parallel lines that form the display of an image projected on a cathode-ray tube of a television set or display screen.
A bitmap image, consisting of a grid of pixels, stored as a sequence of lines.
To scan in parallel lines.
Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.
The gaseous state of a substance that is normally a solid or liquid.
Something insubstantial, fleeting, or transitory; unreal fancy; vain imagination; idle talk; boasting.
Any medicinal agent designed for administration in the form of inhaled vapour.
(in the plural) Hypochondria; melancholy; the blues; hysteria, or other nervous disorder.
Wind; flatulence.
To become vapor; to be emitted or circulated as vapor.
To turn into vapor.
definitions.example: to vapor away a heated fluid

definitions.definition: To emit vapor or fumes.
To use insubstantial language; to boast or bluster.
To give (someone) the vapors; to depress, to bore.
A common gambling card game in casinos, where the object is to get as close to 21 without going over.
A hand in the game of blackjack consisting of a face card and an ace.
The flag (i.e., a jack) traditionally flown by pirate ships; popularly thought to be a white skull and crossed bones on a black field (the Jolly Roger).

A small, flat, blunt, usually leather-covered weapon loaded with heavy material such as lead or ball bearings, intended to inflict a blow to the head that renders the victim unconscious with diminished risk of lasting cranial trauma.
Any of several species of weed of genus Bidens, such as Bidens pilosa, in the family Compositae.
A blackjack oak.
To strike with a blackjack or similar weapon.
An animal, such as a dairy cow, kept for the milk it produces.
A person who milks.
A milking machine.
A conservative poker player who only raises the stakes on a good hand.
(chiefly in the plural) A woman’s breast.
(of a thing) Having a low temperature.
definitions.example: A cold wind whistled through the trees.

definitions.definition: (of the weather) Causing the air to be cold.
definitions.example: The forecast is that it will be very cold today.

definitions.definition: (of a person or animal) Feeling the sensation of coldness, especially to the point of discomfort.
definitions.example: She was so cold she was shivering.

definitions.definition: Unfriendly, emotionally distant or unfeeling.
definitions.example: She shot me a cold glance before turning her back.

definitions.definition: Dispassionate, not prejudiced or partisan, impartial.
definitions.example: He's a nice guy, but the cold facts say we should fire him.

definitions.definition: Completely unprepared; without introduction.
definitions.example: He was assigned cold calls for the first three months.

definitions.definition: Unconscious or deeply asleep; deprived of the metaphorical heat associated with life or consciousness.
definitions.example: After one more beer he passed out cold.

definitions.definition: (usually with "have" or "know" transitively) Perfectly, exactly, completely; by heart.
definitions.example: Keep that list in front of you, or memorize it cold.

definitions.definition: (usually with "have" transitively) Cornered, done for.
definitions.example: Criminal interrogation. Initially they will dream up explanations faster than you could ever do so, but when they become fatigued, often they will acknowledge that you have them cold.

definitions.definition: Not pungent or acrid.
Unexciting; dull; uninteresting.
Affecting the sense of smell (as of hunting dogs) only feebly; having lost its odour.
definitions.example: a cold scent

definitions.definition: Not sensitive; not acute.
Distant; said, in the game of hunting for some object, of a seeker remote from the thing concealed. Compare warm and hot.
definitions.example: You're cold … getting warmer … hot! You've found it!

definitions.definition: Having a bluish effect; not warm in colour.
Rarely used or accessed, and thus able to be relegated to slower storage.
Without compassion; heartless; ruthless
definitions.example: I can't believe she said that...that was cold!

definitions.definition: The therapeutic use of ionizing radiation, almost exclusively used for the treatment of malignant disease.
Physical injury; hurt; damage
definitions.example: No harm came to my possessions.

definitions.definition: Emotional or figurative hurt
definitions.example: Although not physically injured in the car accident, she received some psychological harm.

definitions.definition: Detriment; misfortune.
definitions.example: I wish him no harm.

definitions.definition: That which causes injury, damage, or loss.
To cause injury to another; to hurt; to cause damage to something.
The act of paying.
A sum of money paid in exchange for goods or services.
A legendary serpentine or reptilian creature.
An animal of various species that resemble a dragon in appearance:
(with definite article, often capitalized) The constellation Draco.
A fierce and unpleasant woman; a harridan.
definitions.example: She’s a bit of a dragon.

definitions.definition: (with definite article, often capitalized) The (historical) Chinese empire or the People's Republic of China.
definitions.example: Napoleon already warned of the awakening of the Dragon.

definitions.definition: Something very formidable or dangerous.
A type of playing-tile (red dragon, green dragon, white dragon) in the game of mahjong.
A luminous exhalation from marshy ground, seeming to move through the air like a winged serpent.
A short musket hooked to a swivel attached to a soldier's belt; so called from a representation of a dragon's head at the muzzle.
A background process similar to a daemon.
A variety of carrier pigeon.
A transvestite man, or more broadly a male-to-female transgender person.
A 42.195 kilometre (26 mile 385 yard) road race.
(by extension) Any extended or sustained activity.
definitions.example: He had a cleaning marathon the night before his girlfriend came over.

definitions.definition: To run a marathon.
To watch or read a large number of instalments of (a film, book, TV series, etc.) in one sitting.
definitions.example: We're going to marathon Star Trek next weekend.

definitions.definition: To record in a journal.
To keep a journal.
A dessert of British origin containing stewed fruit topped with a crumbly mixture of fat, flour, and sugar.
definitions.example: blackberry and apple crumble

definitions.definition: To fall apart; to disintegrate.
definitions.example: The empire crumbled when the ruler's indiscretions came to light.

definitions.definition: To break into crumbs.
definitions.example: We crumbled some bread into the water.

definitions.definition: To mix (ingredients such as flour and butter) in such a way as to form crumbs.
definitions.example: Using your fingers, crumble the ingredients with the fingertips, lifting in an upward motion, until the mixture is sandy and resembles large breadcrumbs.

definitions.definition: One who engages in a raid; a plunderer.
A person who takes or attempts to take control of a firm against the will of current management by purchasing a controlling interest of stock and acquiring proxies.
A special forces operative; a commando.
A warship which is light, maneuverable, and fast-moving.
A person who uncovers evidence of improper behavior within governmental or private organizations.
A lazy person.
Sloth (animal).
To laze, act in a lazy manner.
Unwilling to do work or make an effort; disinclined to exertion.
definitions.example: Get out of bed, you lazy lout!

definitions.definition: Causing or characterised by idleness; relaxed or leisurely.
definitions.example: I love staying inside and reading on a lazy Sunday.

definitions.definition: Showing a lack of effort or care.
definitions.example: lazy writing

definitions.definition: Sluggish; slow-moving.
definitions.example: We strolled along beside a lazy stream.

definitions.definition: Lax:
(of a cattle brand) Turned so that (the letter) is horizontal instead of vertical.
Employing lazy evaluation; not calculating results until they are immediately required.
definitions.example: a lazy algorithm

definitions.definition: Wicked; vicious.
To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
Subjected to kidnapping
A small mechanically produced piece (chip) of wood, generally from 0.5 to 10 cm in diameter, used primarily as raw material for pulp, paper and construction boards, as well as fuel and mulch.
A small fibre of wood; especially such material as used to make ingrain wallpaper, aka woodchip wallpaper. See Wikipedia article on ingrain wallpaper.
Great or overwhelming beauty or splendour.
Honour, admiration, or distinction, accorded by common consent to a person or thing; high reputation; renown.
That quality in a person or thing which secures general praise or honour.
Worship or praise.

An optical phenomenon, consisting of concentric rings and somewhat similar to a rainbow, caused by sunlight or moonlight interacting with the water droplets that compose mist or clouds, centered on the antisolar or antilunar point.
Victory; success.
An emanation of light supposed to shine from beings that are specially holy. It is represented in art by rays of gold, or the like, proceeding from the head or body, or by a disk, or a mere line.
The manifestation of the presence of God as perceived by humans in Abrahamic religions.
Pride; boastfulness; arrogance.
To exult with joy; to rejoice.
To boast; to be proud.
To shine radiantly.
(of hair) Uncombed; dishevelled.
(by extension) Disorderly; untidy; messy; not kept up.
Rough; unpolished
Lack of the ability to do something; incapability.
Lack of the option to do something; powerlessness.
An impossibility
definitions.example: Late 14th century: “Madame,” quod he, “this were an impossible!” — Geoffrey Chaucer, ‘The Franklin's Tale’, Canterbury Tales

definitions.definition: Not possible; not able to be done or happen.
definitions.example: It is difficult, if not impossible, to memorize 20,000 consecutive numbers.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Very difficult to deal with.
definitions.example: You never listen to a word I say – you're impossible!

definitions.definition: Imaginary
definitions.example: impossible quantities, or imaginary numbers

definitions.definition: An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.
An intersection of data in two or more database tables.
The act of joining something, such as a network.
The lowest upper bound, an operation between pairs of elements in a lattice, denoted by the symbol ∨.
To connect or combine into one; to put together.
definitions.example: The plumber joined the two ends of the broken pipe.

definitions.definition: To come together; to meet.
definitions.example: Parallel lines never join.

definitions.definition: To come into the company of.
definitions.example: I will join you watching the football game as soon as I have finished my work.

definitions.definition: To become a member of.
definitions.example: Many children join a sports club.

definitions.definition: To produce an intersection of data in two or more database tables.
definitions.example: By joining the Customer table on the Product table, we can show each customer's name alongside the products they have ordered.

definitions.definition: To unite in marriage.
To enjoin upon; to command.
To accept, or engage in, as a contest.
definitions.example: to join encounter, battle, or issue

definitions.definition: One who twists.
The instrument used in twisting, or making twists.
A ball delivered with a twist, as in cricket or billiards.
A tornado.
A girder
The inner part of the thigh, the proper place to rest upon when on horseback.
A crook, a villain.
The party game Twister, usually capitalized, or a variant.
The act of vibrating or the condition of being vibrated.
Any periodic process, especially a rapid linear motion of a body about an equilibrium position.
A single complete vibrating motion.
An instinctively sensed emotional aura or atmosphere; vibes.
A usually plastic card which stores a digital signature that is used to operate an electronic access control lock.
One who performs for, or entertains, an audience.
One who performs or does anything.
definitions.example: the performer of an action

definitions.definition: Doubt; the condition of being uncertain or without conviction.
Something uncertain or ambiguous.
A parameter that measures the dispersion of a range of measured values.
Inverted; turned so that the top is at the bottom.
definitions.example: The pattern resembled an upside down letter W.

definitions.definition: In great disorder.
Owing more money for something than it is worth; having negative equity.
definitions.example: He's upside down on his mortgage.

definitions.definition: Inverted, so that the top is now at the bottom.
definitions.example: The Union flag was flying upside down, a sign of danger.

definitions.definition: In great disorder.
definitions.example: The thief had turned the room upside down.

definitions.definition: Underwear covering the genitalia and often buttocks, usually going no higher than the navel.
Anything that seems to be something that it is not.
definitions.example: Using artificial additives, scientists can create the illusion of fruit flavours in food.

definitions.definition: A misapprehension; a belief in something that is in fact not true.
definitions.example: Jane has this illusion that John is in love with her.

definitions.definition: A magician’s trick.
The state of being deceived or misled.
A sight or view of the interior of anything; a deep inspection or view; introspection; frequently used with into.
Power of acute observation and deduction
Knowledge (usually derived from consumer understanding) that a company applies in order to make a product or brand perform better and be more appealing to customers
Intuitive apprehension of the inner nature of a thing or things; intuition.
An extended understanding of a subject resulting from identification of relationships and behaviors within a model, context, or scenario.
An individual's awareness of the nature and severity of one's mental illness.
A girl or an unmarried young woman; maiden.
A female servant or cleaner (short for maidservant).
A virgin, now female but originally one of either gender.
A lawyer.
A male member of the gentry ranking below a knight.
An honorific sometimes placed after a man's name.
A gentleman who attends or escorts a lady in public.
A squire; a youth who in the hopes of becoming a knight attended upon a knight
A shield-bearer, but also applied to other attendants.
To attend, wait on, escort.
That can be justified.
In a rough manner; without kindness, softness, or gentleness.
Unevenly or irregularly.
Without precision or exactness; imprecise but close to in quantity or amount; approximately.
To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.
definitions.example: The disaster shocked the world.

definitions.definition: To give an electric shock to.
To meet with a shock; to collide in a violent encounter.
To collect, or make up, into a shock or shocks; to stook.
definitions.example: to shock rye

definitions.definition: Surprised, startled, confused, or taken aback.
Suffering from shock.
definitions.example: Patient is shocked.

definitions.definition: Silly, quirky
Belonging to, or proceeding from the original stock; native
Not counterfeit, spurious, false, or adulterated
definitions.example: a genuine text;  a genuine production;  genuine materials; genuine friendship

definitions.definition: Someone who devises or suggests a project; a proposer or planner of something.
An optical device that projects a beam of light, especially one used to project an image (or moving images) onto a screen.
One who projects, or ascribes his/her own feelings to others.
An operator that forms a projection.
A pretty person; a term of address to a pretty person.
Something that is pretty.
definitions.example: We'll stop at the knife store and look at the sharp pretties.

definitions.definition: To make pretty; to beautify
Pleasant to the sight or other senses; attractive, especially of women or children, but less strikingly than something beautiful.
Of objects or things: nice-looking, appealing.
Fine-looking; only superficially attractive; initially appealing but having little substance; see petty.
Cunning; clever, skilful.
Moderately large; considerable.
Excellent, commendable, pleasing; fitting or proper (of actions, thoughts etc.).
Awkward, unpleasant.
Somewhat, fairly, quite; sometimes also (by meiosis) very.
Prettily, in a pretty manner.
A style of bowling in which a (right-handed) bowler spins the ball such that, after pitching, it moves from off to leg (for a right-handed batsman).
Any of the Old World mammals of the family Bovidae, such as the Cape buffalo, Syncerus caffer, or the water buffalo Bubalus bubalis.
A related North American animal, the American bison, Bison bison.
A buffalo robe.
The buffalo fish.
To hunt buffalo.
To outwit, confuse, deceive, or intimidate.
To pistol-whip.
A homogeneous mass of rock, not layered and without an obvious crystal structure.
definitions.example: karst massives in western Georgia

definitions.definition: A group of people from a locality, or sharing a collective aim, interest, etc.
definitions.example: Big up to the Croydon massive!

definitions.definition: Very large in size or extent
definitions.example: Compared to its counterparts from World War II, the Abrams main battle tank is truly massive.

definitions.definition: Very large or bulky and heavy and solid
definitions.example: A massive comet or asteroid appears to have ended the era of the dinosaurs.

definitions.definition: To a very great extent; total, utter.
Of particularly exceptional quality or value; awesome.
definitions.example: Did you see Colbert last night? He was massive!

definitions.definition: Outstanding, beautiful
definitions.example: Your dress is massive, love. Where did you get it?

definitions.definition: Affecting a large portion of the body, or severe.
definitions.example: a massive heart attack

definitions.definition: Having a large mass
(of a particle) Possessing mass.
definitions.example: Some bosons are massive while others are massless.

definitions.definition: Homogenous, unstructured
To imitate, especially in order to ridicule.
To take on the appearance of another, for protection or camouflage.
Mimicry
Resembling the metal copper, especially in color.
A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.
definitions.example: There are 2 churches and 3 shops in our village.

definitions.definition: A rural habitation that has a church, but no market.
A planned community such as a retirement community or shopping district.
A gated community.
To place; to set in a particular spot or position.
To find out where something is located.
To designate the site or place of; to define the limits of (Note: the designation may be purely descriptive: it need not be prescriptive.)
definitions.example: The council must locate the new hospital

definitions.definition: To place oneself; to take up one's residence; to settle.
Arrival at the shore by ship.
definitions.example: Lighthouses are usually the first aids seen when making landfall.

definitions.definition: The point at which a hurricane or similar storm reaches land.
The first land discovered after a sea voyage.
A landslip.
